task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenMRS SPA dashboard or home page is visible, indicating a successful login.
The Appointments module, scheduling interface, or calendar view is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Select 'Super User' as the provider for the new schedule block.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the schedule creation form or modal, 'Super User' is entered or selected in the provider field.
In the schedule creation form, 'General Medicine' is entered or selected in the service or appointment type field.

task_completion.description: Set the schedule block for tomorrow from 09:00 to 17:00.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The start time is explicitly set to '09:00', the end time is set to '17:00', and the date field reflects tomorrow's date.

task_completion.description: Save the schedule block and verify its creation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification is displayed, or the newly created schedule block for 'Super User' (09:00 - 17:00) is visible on the Appointments calendar.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used a web browser to interact with the OpenMRS application.
integrity.visual_evidence: A web browser window (e.g., Chromium, Firefox) is visible with the OpenMRS URL in the address bar.

integrity.description: Verify the schedule was created using the OpenMRS graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the OpenMRS UI forms rather than using a terminal, cURL commands, or a script to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the final result.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser developer tools (Inspect Element) are not open to manipulate the DOM for a fake success message or calendar entry.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Provider Name must be exactly 'Super User'. Service Name must be exactly 'General Medicine'. Start Time must be exactly '09:00'. End Time must be exactly '17:00'. The schedule must be created for the day after the system date (tomorrow).
